name: expurge
description: Find where your personal data appears on people-search sites and generate opt-out requests. Nothing leaves your device.

_notes.webNavigation: Removed — was reserved for background webNavigation.onErrorOccurred (load-error skipping) but never wired. Re-add to permissions when M9 wires webNavigation.onErrorOccurred.
_notes.dataset_update_host: https://data.expurge.dev/* is OPTIONAL, not required: the signed-dataset fetch (M7) is consent-gated, so the host permission is requested via browser.permissions.request() at opt-in time (Settings → Broker data updates), never held by default. Origin is single-sourced as DATASET_HOST_PATTERN in src/shared/dataset.ts — change BOTH together. Host is data.expurge.dev, matching the extension id (expurge@expurge.dev) — no id reconcile needed before AMO.
_notes.m9_new_brokers: Each new broker domain needs TWO entries: (1) optional_host_permissions so browser.permissions.request() can offer it at run start; (2) content_scripts.matches so the headless challenge reporter injects (challenge detection). Both are required — either alone does not work. Verdict UI lives in the window-level sidebar (sidebar_action), which needs no per-domain entry.
_notes.m8_no_new_permissions: Persistence opt-ins (M8) use storage.local only — no new permissions needed.
_notes.firefox_ignores_underscore_keys: Firefox logs a warning for unknown top-level keys but loads the extension. Remove _notes before AMO submission if it causes review friction.
